title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

seo.title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken Recipe - Crispy & Spicy
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make the best Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken at home! This crispy, sweet, and spicy recipe brings authentic Korean flavors to your kitchen. Get the full step-by-step guide.

sweet and spicy Korean fried chicken recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken Recipe
h2Headings: What is Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A plate of crispy Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken coated in a glossy sweet and spicy sauce, garnished with sesame seeds and green onions.
seo.imageCaption: Get ready to indulge in the ultimate crispy, sweet, and spicy Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken! Perfect for game night or a delicious family dinner.

description: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a beloved dish featuring incredibly crispy chicken coated in a luscious, sweet, and spicy glaze. This iconic Korean comfort food offers a delightful crunch followed by a burst of savory, tangy, and subtly fiery flavors. It is perfect for sharing and a true testament to Korean culinary artistry.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Pat the chicken pieces very dry with paper towels. Season them generously with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and ginger powder. In a large bowl, combine the potato starch and all-purpose flour. In a separate small bowl, whisk the egg.

instructions.title: Create the Batter
instructions.text: Dip each seasoned chicken piece into the whisked egg, then dredge it thoroughly in the starch-flour mixture, ensuring it is fully coated. For an extra crispy coating, you can lightly mist the coated chicken with water and dredge it again in the dry mixture.

instructions.title: First Fry the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat enough frying oil in a deep pot or Dutch oven to 160C (325F). Carefully add the chicken pieces in batches, making sure not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for 6-8 minutes until light golden brown and cooked through. Remove the chicken and place it on a wire rack to drain excess oil.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce
instructions.text: While the chicken rests, prepare the sauce. In a saucepan, combine gochujang, soy sauce, rice vinegar, honey or corn syrup, brown sugar, minced garlic, grated ginger, sesame oil, and 2 tablespoons of water. Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat, stirring constantly until it thickens slightly and becomes glossy, about 3-5 minutes. Remove from heat.

instructions.title: Second Fry for Ultimate Crispiness
instructions.text: Increase the oil temperature to 175C (350F). Return the partially fried chicken to the hot oil in batches. Fry for another 3-4 minutes until the chicken is deeply golden brown and exceptionally crispy. This double-frying step is key for authentic Korean fried chicken texture.

instructions.title: Coat the Chicken
instructions.text: Immediately transfer the hot, double-fried chicken pieces into the saucepan with the prepared Yangnyeom sauce. Toss quickly and thoroughly until all the chicken pieces are evenly coated with the sticky, glossy sauce. Work quickly to ensure the chicken remains crispy.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the coated Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken to a serving platter. Garnish generously with toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions. Serve immediately and enjoy the perfect balance of sweet, spicy, and savory flavors with an incredible crunch.

tips: Double frying the chicken is absolutely crucial for achieving that signature crispy, crackly crust that Korean fried chicken is famous for. Do not skip this step!
For the crispiest results, use potato starch for the batter. If unavailable, cornstarch is a good substitute, but potato starch offers a superior crunch.

Serve Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken immediately after coating to enjoy its optimal crispiness. Pair it with pickled radishes (chicken-mu) for a traditional Korean experience and to cut through the richness.
article: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a beloved Korean Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
